name: hotpatch
title: hotpatch

commands.name: hotpatcher

arguments.description: Enable verbose logging. Add more 'v's for more
arguments.raw: -v[vvvv]     Enable verbose logging. Add more 'v's for more

arguments.description: Dry run. Do not modify anything in process
arguments.raw: -N           Dry run. Do not modify anything in process

arguments.description: Path or name of the .so file to load. Switches off execution pointer reset
arguments.raw: -l <.so>     Path or name of the .so file to load. Switches off execution pointer reset

arguments.description: Symbol to invoke during the dll inject. Optional.
arguments.raw: -s <name>    Symbol to invoke during the dll inject. Optional.

arguments.description: Set execution pointer to symbol. Cannot be set with -s option
arguments.raw: -x <name>    Set execution pointer to symbol. Cannot be set with -s option

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# hotpatcher -h
 
 Usage: hotpatcher [options] <PID of process to patch>
 
 Options:
 -h           This help message
 -V           Version number.
 -v[vvvv]     Enable verbose logging. Add more 'v's for more
 -N           Dry run. Do not modify anything in process
 -l <.so>     Path or name of the .so file to load. Switches off execution pointer reset
 -s <name>    Symbol to invoke during the dll inject. Optional.
 -x <name>    Set execution pointer to symbol. Cannot be set with -s option

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=652a5af4e14f2fb7c493eac4aa160077a05a0e3d, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Hot patches Linux executables with .so file injection
Hotpatch is a library that can be used to dynamically load a
shared library (.so) file on Linux from one process into
another already running process, without affecting the
execution of the target process. The API is a C API, but
also supported in C++.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use hotpatcher because ### hotpatch.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hotpatcher [options] <PID of process to patch>` as documented upstream for hotpatcher.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair hotpatcher with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `hotpatcher --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install hotpatch. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `hotpatcher [options] <PID of process to patch>`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### hotpatch.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install hotpatch
Install with: sudo apt install hotpatch
